summary: Kaiming He compares the current state of generative modeling to the 'pre-AlexNet' era of computer vision, arguing that today's diffusion and autoregressive models rely on layer-wise training rather than true end-to-end optimization. To bridge this gap, he introduces 'Mean Flows,' a framework that predicts average velocity rather than instantaneous velocity, enabling high-quality, one-step generative modeling. He challenges the community to find the 'AlexNet counterpart' for generative models that fully respects inference-time behavior during training.

principles.statement: Recognition and generation are two sides of the same coin.
principles.rationale: Recognition is an abstraction process that flows from a complex data manifold to a simple embedding/label manifold. Generation is the exact reverse, flowing from an unstructured embedding (noise) to concretize a data point.
principles.source_quote: the problem of recognition and the problem of generation are just the uh two sides of the same coin.

principles.statement: Today's generative models are conceptually trained layer-wise, not end-to-end.
principles.rationale: Models like diffusion or autoregressive networks have multi-step computational graphs at inference time, but during training, they only optimize one time step (or token) at a time. This mirrors the pre-AlexNet era of training deep networks one layer at a time.
principles.source_quote: today's most successful genative models are conceptually a kind of layer wise training if we compare it with um the classification models um before after uh the Alex net area.

principles.statement: Ground truth flow fields exist independently of neural networks.
principles.rationale: The velocity field mapping an input distribution to an output distribution is a mathematical reality defined by the distributions and the interpolation method. Neural networks merely approximate this pre-existing field.
principles.source_quote: this ground truth flow field is it doesn't depends on any neuronet networks. It can provide a ground truth for neuronet network to learn but it is something that um that has nothing to do with any neuronet networks

principles.statement: Diffusion models are a special case of flow matching.
principles.rationale: Flow matching is a broader framework for constructing trajectories between distributions, and diffusion models represent just one specific instantiation of this concept.
principles.source_quote: diffusion model is a special case of flow matching model which is a special case of general flows which is a special case of uh generative models.

frameworks.name: Mean Flows Algorithm
frameworks.when_to_apply: When training a generative model to perform one-step (or few-step) generation by learning the average velocity between two time steps instead of the instantaneous velocity.
steps: Define a neural network function to predict the average velocity, conditioned on two time variables (start and end).
Sample a batch of data, time steps, and noise.
Perform interpolation between data and noise to compute the instantaneous conditional velocity.
Compute the time derivative of the velocity using a Jacobian Vector Product (JVP).
Modify the regression target using the mean flow identity (combining instantaneous velocity and the time derivative).
Optimize the network using a conditional flow matching objective.
frameworks.source_quote: conceptually it's just two line modification of the original flow matching algorithm

mental_models.name: The Pre-AlexNet Analogy for Generative Models
mental_models.description: Viewing the current paradigm of training diffusion and autoregressive models as analogous to the pre-2012 era of computer vision. Just as researchers used to train deep networks one layer at a time (e.g., denoising autoencoders) before AlexNet popularized end-to-end backpropagation, today's generative models are trained one time-step at a time rather than optimizing the full multi-step inference trajectory.
mental_models.example: Comparing the training of a diffusion model (predicting noise for a single time step and ignoring the rest of the generation chain) to freezing layers in a Deep Belief Network.

mental_models.name: Neural ODEs as ResNets
mental_models.description: The realization that while Neural ODEs are theoretically formulated as continuous integration over time, practical computational constraints force them to be implemented as finite sums. Therefore, a Neural ODE in practice is conceptually just another ResNet.
mental_models.example: When building a Neural ODE to solve an integral, the actual operation performed by the computer is a finite sum addition, mirroring the discrete time operation of a ResNet.

mental_models.name: Instantaneous vs. Average Velocity
mental_models.description: Borrowing from physics, instantaneous velocity only describes the tangent direction at a single point in time. To take large steps (like one-step generation), one must use average velocity, which characterizes the total displacement between a starting point and an end point.
mental_models.example: Using average velocity to jump directly from pure noise (t=1) to a clean image (t=0) in a single step, rather than taking hundreds of tiny steps following the instantaneous tangent.

heuristics: In theory we want to do integral, but in practice we can only do finite sum.
If you have a good library, doing JVP (Jacobian Vector Product) is not much more complicated than doing VJP (backpropagation).

signature_quotes.text: the problem of recognition and the problem of generation are just the uh two sides of the same coin.
signature_quotes.context: Explaining how recognition maps data to embeddings, while generation maps embeddings to data.

signature_quotes.text: today's most successful genative models are conceptually a kind of layer wise training if we compare it with um the classification models um before after uh the Alex net area.
signature_quotes.context: Critiquing the current training paradigm of diffusion and autoregressive models.

signature_quotes.text: we want to do integration but what we are doing is still a finite sum.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing the practical implementation of Neural ODEs.

signature_quotes.text: in the area of generative modeling are we still in in in the area of uh um pre Alex stage?
signature_quotes.context: Concluding the talk by challenging the audience to find true end-to-end training methods for generative models.

signature_quotes.text: this ground truth flow field is it doesn't depends on any neuronet networks. It can provide a ground truth for neuronet network to learn but it is something that um that has nothing to do with any neuronet networks
signature_quotes.context: Explaining the mathematical foundation of flow matching.

anti_patterns.description: Training generative models by only optimizing one time step at a time while ignoring the full inference-time computational graph.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It fails to fully respect the model's inference-time behavior, requiring many iterative steps during generation and lacking the benefits of true end-to-end optimization.

anti_patterns.description: Using instantaneous velocity to characterize large time steps or one-step generation.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Instantaneous velocity only describes the tangent direction at a single point. It is not precise for characterizing the displacement over a large time interval, leading to errors if used for one-step generation.
